---
title: "Modeling and software details"
subtitle: "Technical reference for the stLMM model grammar and implementation"
author: "Andrew O. Finley"
description: "Technical reference for stLMM notation, model terms, likelihoods, collapsed sampling, sparse linear algebra, recovery, and prediction."
format:
  html:
    toc: false
    number-sections: false
    code-fold: show
    code-overflow: wrap
    html-math-method: katex
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
---

The package vignettes focus on runnable examples. The technical reference provides a more complete description of the model notation, likelihoods, structured process terms, collapsed sampler, sparse linear algebra, recovery, and prediction machinery used by `stLMM`.

[Download the PDF technical reference](../stLMM-modeling-and-software-details.pdf).

The document is intended for readers who want implementation-level detail beyond the user-facing examples. It is most useful when checking how a model formula maps to the latent process representation, how the collapsed sampler is constructed, or how recovery and prediction use the sampled parameters and retained process draws.
